event.message: 正在请求 AI 规划

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
When a tab has pageSample status ok, treat pageSample title, metaDescription, headings, contentKind, and visibleText as the strongest content signals. Use them to disambiguate generic tab titles and sanitized URLs.
Do not send a sampled tab to review just because its title or URL is generic; use review only when the pageSample content itself is mixed, sensitive, or still unclear.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: high, spend more reasoning on semantic grouping.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"agent_benchmark_research","title":"Agent Benchmark Research","color":"purple","confidence":0.86,"ids":[10000,10002,10008,10009,10019],"reason":"These tabs form one benchmark research stream: AI coding agents, automated code review benchmarks, and evaluation methodology such as leaderboards, datasets, and scoring."},{"key":"chrome_extension_build","title":"Chrome Extension Build","color":"blue","confidence":0.84,"ids":[10001,10004,10006,10010,10012,10014,10018,10020,10021],"reason":"These tabs support building the extension: Chrome APIs, side panel/frontend implementation, UI pattern references, testing/debugging, and an extension tutorial resource."},{"key":"worker_gateway_backend","title":"Worker Gateway Backend","color":"green","confidence":0.97,"ids":[10003,10011,10013],"reason":"These tabs all cover the Cloudflare Worker gateway backend, including routing, secrets, tunnel diagnostics, quotas, and abuse prevention."},{"key":"database_sql_work","title":"Database & SQL Work","color":"red","confidence":0.95,"ids":[10005,10022],"reason":"These tabs are focused on database debugging and planning, specifically migrations, rollback safety, index strategy, and SQL performance."},{"key":"background_reading_talks","title":"Background Reading & Talks","color":"grey","confidence":0.79,"ids":[10015,10016,10023],"reason":"These tabs are saved background materials for later consumption: long-form product reading, a talk transcript, and a reference page."}],"review":[{"id":10007,"reason":"Shopping/finance content can include billing, checkout, or account details; under the conservative preset this should be reviewed manually."},{"id":10017,"reason":"Finance/pricing content is potentially sensitive and should be reviewed manually instead of auto-grouped."}]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Organizing project tasks**

I'm thinking about how to group useful tasks across different domains rather than sticking to a domain-only approach. For instance, I could combine "Chrome extension APIs," "Frontend implementation," and "Product UI design" under a broader task like "Chrome extension product build" since they interrelate. However, since there are distinct subtopics, I want to avoid making it too broad. I also see a potential connection between "AI coding agents" and "LLM evaluation research," so I might group those too.

preview.reviewGroupReason: AI 暂时拿不准这些页面的共同主题，不会硬塞进其他分组。

groups.title: Agent Benchmark Research
groups.reason: These tabs form one benchmark research stream: AI coding agents, automated code review benchmarks, and evaluation methodology such as leaderboards, datasets, and scoring.

groups.title: Chrome Extension Build
groups.reason: These tabs support building the extension: Chrome APIs, side panel/frontend implementation, UI pattern references, testing/debugging, and an extension tutorial resource.

groups.title: Worker Gateway Backend
groups.reason: These tabs all cover the Cloudflare Worker gateway backend, including routing, secrets, tunnel diagnostics, quotas, and abuse prevention.

groups.title: Database & SQL Work
groups.reason: These tabs are focused on database debugging and planning, specifically migrations, rollback safety, index strategy, and SQL performance.

groups.title: Background Reading & Talks
groups.reason: These tabs are saved background materials for later consumption: long-form product reading, a talk transcript, and a reference page.
